summary: DressVibe backend — NEW 'Aggiungi accessori' feature on POST /api/studio/edit verified end-to-end against the public preview URL with the canonical demo bearer `test_session_screen` → user_demo01. All 6 pytest cases pass (100%). Coverage: (A) regression without accessories returns 200 with `image_base64`, (B) call with 3 accessories (scarpe/borse/occhiali) returns 200, (C) Pydantic validation correctly returns 422 when an accessory item omits either `category` or `image_base64`, (D) empty accessories list `[]` behaves identically to the field being absent (200), (E) when `gen_id` is provided the edited image is appended to the linked generation and the returned `image_index` correctly equals the new last-index of the persisted `images` array (and `thumbs` also grew by 1). Gemini was responsive during this run — every studio/edit call returned 200, no 502 was observed and zero 5xx/Tracebacks appeared in backend logs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown further (~4112 lines). Splitting into routers (auth/, garments/, generations/, studio/, social/) is overdue — the file is well past the 700-line guideline.

studio_edit: refs construction is clean — source image is always position #1 and each accessory is appended only if `acc.image_base64` is truthy, guarding against silently-empty entries. The suffix is then composed with `num_garments=1`, so the per-item line reads `Reference image #2 (...)` etc. Math verified.

studio_edit: the suffix is only appended when at least one accessory had a non-empty image_base64 (`accessory_count > 0`). This correctly makes `accessories=[]` indistinguishable from `accessories=None`, matching brief D.
StudioEditRequest does NOT persist accessories anywhere — they exist only for the duration of the request (disposable one-shot), consistent with the GenerationCreate pattern. Good for DB hygiene.

`gen_id` linkage uses `find_one_and_update` with `return_document=AFTER` and a `projection={'images':1}`, then computes `len(images)-1` — correct and atomic. Verified end-to-end: images & thumbs arrays each grew by exactly +1 after the edit, and `image_index` matches the pre-call length.

Minor: when `image_count` is stored as a denormalised field on the generation doc, the $push update does not increment it. The list endpoint computes `image_count` via aggregation so this is not a bug today, but a future refactor that reads the persisted field directly would drift. Worth a comment in the code or a $inc alongside the $push.
seed_data_creation: An autouse session fixture in /app/backend/tests/test_studio_edit_accessories.py idempotently upserts (a) user_id=user_demo01 in `users` and (b) the bearer token `test_session_screen` in `user_sessions` with a 7-day expiry (no teardown, same approach as iteration_2). For test E (gen_id linkage) the suite creates a temporary `gen_TEST_STUDIO_*` document in `generations` for user_demo01 with one tiny PNG in `images`/`thumbs`, then deletes it on teardown — no pollution remains in the DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_studio_edit_accessories.py -v` (~80s total, makes 4 real Gemini calls). The suite seeds user_demo01 + bearer `test_session_screen` automatically and tolerates either 200 or 502 on every Gemini-dependent call (only schema-validation tests are strict). Companion suite /app/backend/tests/test_accessories.py (iteration_2) still covers the Generation flow and should continue to pass.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects found in this iteration. The new `accessories` field on StudioEditRequest, the refs-list construction in `studio_edit`, the prompt suffix append via `_compose_accessories_suffix(payload.accessories, num_garments=1)`, and the gen_id image_index linkage all behave exactly per the brief.
